---
title: "Examples"
---

Each example below opens in a live [Shinylive](https://shinylive.io/) editor where you can read the source, modify it, and see the result — all in the browser.

<div class="examples-list" style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">

### [Controlled Inputs](../apps/controlled-inputs/index.html?_shinylive-mode=editor-terminal-viewer){target="_blank"}

In most UI frameworks, inputs and state are kept in sync by making the input *controlled* — the input's displayed value is always derived from application state, not managed independently by the browser. nacre brings this pattern to Shiny: bind a reactive value directly to an input's `value` attribute, and every update flows through a single source of truth. This example shows three text inputs all bound to the same `reactiveVal`.

### [Temperature Converter](../apps/temperature/index.html?_shinylive-mode=editor-terminal-viewer){target="_blank"}

A classic reactive UI challenge: two inputs representing the same value in different units, where editing either one should update the other. With nacre's controlled inputs, one `reactiveVal` holds the canonical Celsius value and the Fahrenheit input derives from it via a `reactive()`. Both the number inputs and the vertical sliders stay in sync automatically.

### [Todo List](../apps/todo/index.html?_shinylive-mode=editor-terminal-viewer){target="_blank"}

A TodoMVC-style application demonstrating reactive list management with nacre. Items are stored as a `reactiveVal` holding a plain R list; adding, toggling, and removing items are ordinary functions that update that list. The filter tabs and item count derive reactively from the same source, so everything stays consistent without any manual synchronization.

### [Shiny Modules](../apps/module/index.html?_shinylive-mode=editor-terminal-viewer){target="_blank"}

nacre components compose naturally with standard Shiny modules. Use `nacreOutput()` and `renderNacre()` to embed a nacre component tree inside a module's UI and server functions, exactly like any other Shiny output. This example instantiates two counter modules side by side, each with fully independent state.

### [Optimistic Updates](../apps/optimistic-updates/index.html?_shinylive-mode=editor-terminal-viewer){target="_blank"}

When a user types into a controlled input, nacre applies the change immediately in the browser before the server round-trip completes — an *optimistic update*. If the server responds with a different value, the browser reconciles to the server's version. This keeps the UI feeling instant while still letting the server be the authority on state.

</div>
